message:
test(readme): integration test for Column Manipulation (fd90.186 / br-ein1y)

Adds readme_column_manipulation_compiles_and_runs covering the 6
APIs documented in README "Column Manipulation" (lines 1287-1311),
which previously had zero integration coverage:

- DataFrame.rename_with (closure-based renaming)
- DataFrame.add_prefix / add_suffix (bulk renaming)
- DataFrame.assign_column (append from Vec<Scalar>)
- DataFrame.assign_fn (closure that sees current frame; uses
  FrameError for error coupling — same pattern as fd90.180)
- DataFrame.select_columns (reorder + project)

Test exercises the README's "ratio = revenue / cost" assign_fn
example shape so README + test stay co-evolved.

Found via self-review on the last 3 commits — all 6 methods existed
in fp-frame but had drifted out of integration coverage.

Verified: 25 integration tests + 7 lib tests + 2 doctests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-ebjw","title":"fp-conformance: fixture FP-P2C-011 groupby_median_multikey_order expected insertion-order, plus stale write_excel_bytes import warning","description":"Two independent issues found while driving conformance toward 0 failures:

1. tests::packet_filter_runs_groupby_aggregate_matrix_packet — 1/30 case fails: groupby_median_multikey_order_strict. fixture's expected_series is in input insertion order [(b,2),(a,1),(b,1)]; pandas default groupby([k1,k2]).median() sorts the MultiIndex, so actual output is [(a,1),(b,1),(b,2)] with values [40.0, 50.0, 20.0]. Identical pattern to g90b's groupby_sum_order fix.

2. Compile emits unused-import warning on write_excel_bytes in crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:30 after 989b/mxho replaced the only two callers with write_excel_bytes_with_options. Not a test failure but clippy -D warnings will fail on the next sweep.

Fix: update expected_series to sorted form (index [s:'a'|i:1, s:'b'|i:1, s:'b'|i:2], values [40.0, 50.0, 20.0]). Drop write_excel_bytes from the fp_io use list.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T07:24:33.199089283Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T07:29:37.392605874Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T07:29:37.390798534Z","close_reason":"shipped 8b6fa4b: FP-P2C-011 groupby_median_multikey_order_strict expected_series realigned to pandas-default MultiIndex sort (a,1)/(b,1)/(b,2) with values [40.0, 50.0, 20.0]; dropped stale write_excel_bytes import. packet_filter_runs_groupby_aggregate_matrix_packet: 29/30 -> 30/30 pass. Clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ebue","title":"Add series str encode/decode/translate null hardened coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:57:01.348876799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:57:26.413018940Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:57:26.411173096Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-edjf","title":"[MEDIUM] fp-frame grouped-window code keeps unused agg_name parameter (r4au)","description":"Reviewing 2f80719 (br-frankenpandas-r4au, cod): The new as_index=true path through apply_grouped_rolling and apply_grouped_resample inserts 'let _ = agg_name;' (lines 28026 + 28075 of crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs) to silence the unused-parameter lint after the refactor changed the closure signature. This is a code smell:

- Marking a parameter as 'let _ =' instead of removing it from the function signature signals incomplete refactor and forces every caller to pass a value the body never reads.
- Removes the documentation contract that 'agg_name' was used to identify the aggregation in error messages or evidence ledgers — future maintainers won't know whether to plumb it through or drop it entirely.

Suggested fix: either thread agg_name into the new path (e.g. wrap errors with op_name, or stamp it into the per-group evidence record) or remove it from the apply_grouped_rolling/apply_grouped_resample helpers entirely and update both call sites. Pick one — don't leave _ markers.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22: superseded in-tree. Commit ac1550b (br-frankenpandas-ch4o, fix grouped window fallback schema) removed the agg_name parameter from apply_grouped_rolling entirely (and the sibling apply_grouped_resample site) rather than threading it through, which is the second option the bead proposed. grep 'let _ = agg_name' across fp-frame/src/lib.rs is now empty; closure signatures no longer carry the unused parameter. No follow-up action needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T08:04:04.849224677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:43:34.421409635Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:43:34.417612609Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-ein1y","title":"fd90.186: Integration test for README Column Manipulation section","description":"fd90.186: Add integration test for README "Column Manipulation" section (lines 1287-1311).

The Column Manipulation section documents 6 methods (rename_with, add_prefix, add_suffix, assign_column, assign_fn, select_columns) with zero integration coverage in tests/readme_quick_example.rs. Found during self-review on the last 3 commits.

Scope (single test file, single test function):
- DataFrame.rename_with (closure-based renaming)
- DataFrame.add_prefix / add_suffix
- DataFrame.assign_column (value vector)
- DataFrame.assign_fn (closure that sees current state — already partially covered in apply_and_transform, but spot-check here too)
- DataFrame.select_columns (reorder)

Files: crates/frankenpandas/tests/readme_quick_example.rs (single new test, ~25-35 lines).

Public surface includes Expr enum (the AST), SeriesRef (column reference), EvalContext (evaluation environment), DataFrameExprExt extension trait (df.eval / df.query methods), parse_expr, evaluate / eval_str / filter_dataframe_on_expr / query_str variants, plus Delta + MaterializedView for incremental views. Pure docs. Verify cargo doc cleanly under RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","assignee":"cc-pandas-2","created_at":"2026-04-26T06:09:14.473982397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T06:11:41.371029253Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T06:11:41.368537355Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","fd90","review"]}

DataFrame.to_excel(excel_writer, sheet_name="Data", index=False, index_label="row_id") -> None

Add pandas-style writer options for sheet_name, index, and index_label.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T11:24:31.823288662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T12:09:51.236200705Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T12:09:51.233686024Z","close_reason":"Added ExcelWriteOptions + write_excel_with_options(_bytes) covering pandas sheet_name/index/index_label/header in commit e5af6e5; 5 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -1239,3 +1239,93 @@ fn readme_advanced_selection_compiles_and_runs() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::erro
     assert!(regex_match.column_names().iter().any(|n| n.as_str() == "revenue"));
     Ok(())
 }
+
+/// README "Column Manipulation" section (lines 1287-1311).
+///
+/// Locks in 6 column-management APIs that previously had no integration coverage:
+/// rename_with (closure renaming), add_prefix, add_suffix, assign_column
+/// (value vector), assign_fn (closure form), and select_columns (reorder).
+///
+/// Tracks fd90.186 (br-frankenpandas-ein1y).
+#[test]
+fn readme_column_manipulation_compiles_and_runs() -> Result<(), Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
+    let df = read_csv_str("revenue,cost,units\n1000,400,10\n2000,800,15\n1500,600,12")?;
+
+    // rename_with — closure-driven column renaming.
+    let renamed = df.rename_with(|name| format!("col_{name}"))?;
+    assert!(
+        renamed
+            .column_names()
+            .iter()
+            .all(|n| n.as_str().starts_with("col_"))
+    );
+
+    // add_prefix / add_suffix — bulk renaming.
+    let prefixed = df.add_prefix("input_")?;
+    assert!(
+        prefixed
+            .column_names()
+            .iter()
+            .all(|n| n.as_str().starts_with("input_"))
+    );
+    let suffixed = df.add_suffix("_raw")?;
+    assert!(
+        suffixed
+            .column_names()
+            .iter()
+            .all(|n| n.as_str().ends_with("_raw"))
+    );
+
+    // assign_column — append a computed column from a Vec<Scalar>.
+    let computed: Vec<Scalar> = vec![
+        Scalar::Float64(2.5),
+        Scalar::Float64(2.5),
+        Scalar::Float64(2.5),
+    ];
+    let with_computed = df.assign_column("computed", computed)?;
+    assert!(
+        with_computed
+            .column_names()
+            .iter()
+            .any(|n| n.as_str() == "computed")
+    );
+
+    // assign_fn — closure that sees current DataFrame state.
+    // Mirrors the README's "ratio = revenue / cost" pattern.
+    use frankenpandas::FrameError;
+    let with_ratio = df.assign_fn(vec![(
+        "ratio",
+        Box::new(|d: &DataFrame| -> Result<Column, FrameError> {
+            let rev = d.column("revenue").expect("revenue column");
+            let cost = d.column("cost").expect("cost column");
+            let values: Vec<Scalar> = rev
+                .values()
+                .iter()
+                .zip(cost.values())
+                .map(|(r, c)| match (r, c) {
+                    (Scalar::Int64(a), Scalar::Int64(b)) => {
+                        Scalar::Float64(*a as f64 / *b as f64)
+                    }
+                    _ => Scalar::Null(NullKind::NaN),
+                })
+                .collect();
+            Column::from_values(values).map_err(FrameError::from)
+        }) as Box<dyn Fn(&DataFrame) -> Result<Column, FrameError>>,
+    )])?;
+    assert!(
+        with_ratio
+            .column_names()
+            .iter()
+            .any(|n| n.as_str() == "ratio")
+    );
+
+    // select_columns — reorder + project.
+    let reordered = df.select_columns(&["units", "revenue"])?;
+    let names: Vec<&str> = reordered
+        .column_names()
+        .iter()
+        .map(|n| n.as_str())
+        .collect();
+    assert_eq!(names, vec!["units", "revenue"]);
+    Ok(())
+}
